title: Grassy Brazilian Rice Bake
description: A gooey, comforting lacto-vegetarian dish featuring tomato sauce and tempering chocolate, perfect for a lazy Sunday.
instructions: In a large saucepan, combine the tomato sauce, shredded coconut, unsalted butter, tempering chocolate, firm tofu, onion, garlic, and ground cumin. Stir until the butter has melted.
Bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat, then reduce the heat to low and let it cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the cooked long grain rice to the saucepan and stir to combine.
Transfer the rice mixture to a baking dish and smooth the top.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10-15 minutes, or until the top is lightly browned.
Remove the dish from the oven and let it cool for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired (optional).
